body.title: [sprint] Refactor GoogleSecretary to use Registered Dispatcher pattern
body.intention: I intend that GoogleSecretary is shaped around the Registered Dispatcher pattern from the start, making it extensible without touching dispatch logic.
body.description:
GoogleSecretary (T-google-secretary-device) currently has bespoke routing logic for calendar/email/tasks operations. The Registered Dispatcher pattern (C-registered-dispatcher, §3.10 of TheoryOfOperation.md) is now the rack canonical shape. Refactor GoogleSecretary to use it: capabilities (CalendarHandler, EmailHandler, TasksHandler) self-register at startup; the device dispatcher matches incoming requests to registered handlers by capability tag rather than by explicit conditionals.

Gated on T-google-secretary-device (device must exist first).

**Affected files:** devices/google_secretary/ (shaped correctly from the start)
**Design rules:** Registered Dispatcher (C-registered-dispatcher); orchestration over monolith; external state principle
**Scope boundary:** In scope: shape GoogleSecretary around the three-stack pattern. Out of scope: actual Google API integrations (those live in T-google-secretary-device).
**Completion criteria:** GoogleSecretary capability handlers self-register via register_handler(handles=[...]); no hardcoded capability→handler mapping in dispatcher; a new handler can be added without touching dispatcher code.
